mappingMetadata.description: Mapping of Edexcel 1MA1 GCSE Mathematics syllabus topics to GCSE Knowledge Tree nodes

mappedNodes.reason: Four operations on integers, decimals and fractions
Operations with positive and negative integers
Priority of operations, brackets, powers, roots and reciprocals
HCF, LCM, common factors and multiples
Positive integer powers and associated real roots
Converting between terminating decimals and fractions
Working with fractions in ratio problems
Standard units of mass, length, time, money
Algebraic notation: ab, 3y, a², a³, a²b, a/b, brackets
Substituting numerical values into formulae and expressions
Expressions, equations, formulae, identities, inequalities, terms and factors
Using and interpreting standard mathematical formulae
Rearranging formulae to change the subject
Difference between equation and identity; equivalence and proofs (Higher)
Gradients and intercepts graphically and algebraically
Roots, intercepts, turning points of quadratics
Turning points by completing the square (Higher)
Trig functions sin x, cos x, tan x (Higher)
Equation of tangent at a given point (Higher)

subtopicMappings.subtopicName: Form and solve equations from contexts

mappedNodes.reason: Translate situations into equations and solve
Linear inequalities in one variable, number line representation
Generate terms from term-to-term or position-to-term rule
Arithmetic and geometric progressions, quadratic sequences
nth term of linear (Foundation) and quadratic (Higher) sequences

topicMappings.topicName: Ratio, proportion and rates of change

mappedNodes.reason: Convert between related standard and compound units
Express one quantity as a fraction of another
Ratio notation, reduction to simplest form
Divide a quantity in a given ratio
Multiplicative relationship as ratio or fraction

subtopicMappings.subtopicName: Ratios to fractions and linear functions

mappedNodes.reason: Relate ratios to fractions and linear functions
Speed, rates of pay, unit pricing
Links to similarity and scale factors
Construct equations for direct and inverse proportion (Higher)
Gradient of straight line as rate of change
Gradient at point on curve as instantaneous rate of change (Higher)
Average and instantaneous rate of change (Higher)
Points, lines, vertices, edges, planes, parallel, perpendicular
Perpendicular bisector, bisecting an angle, constructions
Construct figures and solve loci problems
Angles at a point, on straight line, vertically opposite, alternate/corresponding angles, polygon angles
Properties of special quadrilaterals and triangles
SSS, SAS, ASA, RHS congruence criteria
Angle facts, congruence, similarity to derive results
Enlargement including fractional (and negative Higher) scale factors
Centre, radius, chord, diameter, circumference, tangent, arc, sector, segment
Circle theorems concerning angles, radii, tangents and chords (Higher)
Solve geometrical problems on coordinate axes
Faces, surfaces, edges, vertices of 3D shapes
Cubes, cuboids, prisms, cylinders, pyramids, cones, spheres
Volume of cuboids and right prisms
Surface area and volume of spheres, pyramids, cones
Arc lengths, angles and areas of sectors of circles
Sin, cos, tan for right-angled triangles
Exact values of sin, cos, tan for standard angles
Vectors for geometric arguments and proofs (Higher)
Record, describe and analyse frequency of outcomes using tables and frequency trees
Randomness, fairness, equally likely events, expected outcomes
Relative expected frequencies to theoretical probability; 0-1 scale
Probabilities of exhaustive outcomes sum to one; mutually exclusive events
Empirical unbiased samples tend towards theoretical probability with increasing sample size
Theoretical possibility spaces for single and combined experiments
Infer properties of populations from a sample; limitations of sampling
Tables and line graphs for time series
Apply statistics to describe a population
Lines of best fit, interpolation and extrapolation

unmappedDomains.Matrix: Matrix operations are not part of the Edexcel 1MA1 GCSE Mathematics syllabus
unmappedDomains.Differentiation (ALG-DIFF): Calculus/differentiation is not assessed in 1MA1; gradients are estimated geometrically
unmappedDomains.Logarithms (ALG-LOG): Logarithms are not part of the 1MA1 syllabus
unmappedDomains.Set Notation depth (NUM-SN): Set notation is covered in probability contexts (Venn diagrams) but not as a standalone topic in 1MA1
